title: The Constraint Gauntlet
decision: Can this model actually obey hard countable constraints — counting its own words, banning a letter, holding an acrostic — or does it merely gesture at the instruction? A capability prior for constrained generation, and a public, reproducible sensor: every reference answer passes its own grader, every near-miss fails it.

config.system_prompt: You are taking a constrained-writing benchmark. Follow each instruction exactly. Output ONLY the requested text: no preamble, no explanation, no markdown fences, no surrounding quotes.

tasks.summary: Exactly 15 whitespace-separated words about a lighthouse keeper.
tasks.prompt: Describe a lighthouse keeper's morning in exactly 15 words. Words are anything separated by spaces. Output only those 15 words.
tasks.output: He douses the brass lantern, sips black coffee, and watches dawn paint the cold sea.

tasks.summary: Exactly 25 words: a recipe instruction.
tasks.prompt: Write a single cooking instruction for tomato soup in exactly 25 words. Count every whitespace-separated token as one word. Output only the instruction.
tasks.output: Simmer pureed tomatoes, vegetable broth, minced garlic, and heavy cream in a saucepan over medium heat for fifteen minutes, stirring occasionally until completely heated through.

tasks.summary: Exactly 40 words describing a storm at sea.
tasks.prompt: Describe a storm at sea in exactly 40 words. Every whitespace-separated token counts as one word. Output only the description.
tasks.output: Black waves crash against the shivering hull. Howling winds tear the canvas, while jagged lightning splits the bruised sky. Sailors cling to freezing ropes, praying as the relentless ocean threatens to swallow their fragile wooden world into the deep.

tasks.summary: Describe a sunset without the letter e.
tasks.prompt: Describe a sunset in one to three sentences without using the letter "e" anywhere, in any word, uppercase or lowercase. Output only the description.
tasks.output: A crimson sun sinks low, casting warm gold light across a dark, still bay. Soft pink clouds drift by as day turns to night. Dusk falls, and stars start to glow.

tasks.summary: Praise coffee without the letter a.
tasks.prompt: Write two sentences praising coffee without using the letter "a" anywhere, uppercase or lowercase. Output only the two sentences.
tasks.output: This delicious brew offers pure energy to the mind. Every hot cup brings immense joy to my life.

tasks.summary: Five lines whose first letters spell EMBER.
tasks.prompt: Write a five-line poem in which the first letter of line one is E, line two M, line three B, line four E, line five R — spelling EMBER downward. Output only the five lines.
tasks.output:
Each autumn leaf begins to fade and fall,
Myriad colors drift upon the breeze,
Beneath the gray and silent winter sky,
Earth prepares to sleep in frozen dreams,
Resting until the warmth of spring returns.

tasks.summary: Five lines whose first letters spell STONE.
tasks.prompt: Write a five-line poem in which the first letters of the lines spell STONE, in order. Output only the five lines.
tasks.output:
Silent cliffs of ancient gray,
Towering high above the bay,
Overlooking seas that roar,
Never moving from the shore,
Endless through the passing day.

tasks.summary: Exactly four lines, one per season.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly four lines: one line for each season, in order from spring to winter. No blank lines. Output only the four lines.
tasks.output:
Green shoots break through the warming earth as life begins anew.
Golden sunbeams drench the fields in long and lazy afternoons.
Crimson leaves drift slowly down to carpet forest floors.
Silent snow wraps all the world in cold and quiet sleep.

tasks.summary: Exactly seven lines, one per weekday.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly seven lines, one for each day of the week starting Monday, each line naming its day. No blank lines. Output only the seven lines.
tasks.output:
Monday brings the start of a brand new week.
Tuesday arrives as the busy work continues to unfold.
Wednesday marks the middle of our journey toward the weekend.
Thursday builds anticipation as the days quickly slip away.
Friday delivers the welcome relief we have waited for.
Saturday offers a peaceful space to rest and play.
Sunday concludes the cycle with quiet moments of reflection.

tasks.summary: Six lines, each beginning with the word Because.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly six lines, each beginning with the word "Because" followed by a space. No blank lines. Output only the six lines.
tasks.output:
Because the morning sun began to rise, the shadows faded away.
Because the cold wind blew fiercely, we stayed inside by the fire.
Because you listened to my quiet words, I felt truly understood.
Because the path was steep and rocky, we walked with careful steps.
Because we dared to dream of better days, we found the strength to try.
Because the journey is finally complete, we can rest in peace at last.

tasks.summary: Five lines, each beginning with Never.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly five lines of advice, each beginning with the word "Never" followed by a space. No blank lines. Output only the five lines.
tasks.output:
Never compromise your integrity for temporary gains or fleeting approval.
Never underestimate the power of small, consistent steps toward your goals.
Never let the fear of failure prevent you from trying something new.
Never forget to show gratitude to those who support you along the way.
Never allow the opinions of others to define your self-worth and potential.

tasks.summary: A sentence of 8+ words, every word starting with s.
tasks.prompt: Write one sentence of at least eight words in which every word begins with the letter "s". Use no punctuation except one final period. Output only the sentence.
tasks.output: Seven serious sisters silently spun soft silver silk.

tasks.summary: A sentence of 8+ words, every word starting with b.
tasks.prompt: Write one sentence of at least eight words in which every word begins with the letter "b". Use no punctuation except one final period. Output only the sentence.
tasks.output: Beautiful brown bears briskly build big birch barriers.

tasks.summary: A short story whose final words are fixed.
tasks.prompt: Write a story of two to four sentences that ends with exactly this sentence: "Then the lamp went out." Output only the story.
tasks.output: For hours, Clara sat huddled beneath the heavy blankets, listening to the relentless scratching against her bedroom window. She kept her eyes fixed on the flickering wick of her bedside light, praying the fragile flame would ward off whatever was waiting in the dark. Then the lamp went out.

tasks.summary: A story whose first words are fixed.
tasks.prompt: Write a story of two to four sentences that begins with exactly these words: "The door had been painted shut for years". Output only the story.

expectation.value: (?s)\AThe door had been painted shut for years.+\z

tasks.output: The door had been painted shut for years, its edges sealed by thick, yellowed layers of enamel that felt as hard as bone. When Arthur finally drove the chisel into the seam, the wood groaned and gave way with a sound like a dry cough. From the darkness beyond, something wet and heavy slid against the other side of the wood, eager to finally be let out.

tasks.summary: Exactly one sentence: no internal periods.
tasks.prompt: Describe a city waking up in exactly one sentence, ending with a single period and containing no other periods. Output only the sentence.
tasks.output: As the first trolley clangs along the avenue, golden sunlight spills over the rooftops, coaxing sleepy shopkeepers to sweep their doorsteps while the aroma of fresh espresso begins to drift through the awakening streets.

tasks.summary: A warning sign in capitals only.
tasks.prompt: Write a two-sentence warning sign about a sleeping dragon using only capital letters (punctuation and spaces allowed, no lowercase letters anywhere). Output only the sign.
tasks.output: SILENCE IS YOUR ONLY PROTECTION AS THE BEAST SLUMBERS WITHIN. DO NOT MAKE A SOUND OR YOU WILL BECOME ITS NEXT MEAL.

tasks.summary: Exactly 12 words AND no letter e.
tasks.prompt: Describe rain in exactly 12 words without using the letter "e" anywhere. Words are whitespace-separated tokens. Output only the description.
tasks.output: Soft drops fall from clouds, pouring down on dry dusty ground.

tasks.summary: Acrostic RAIN, every line all-caps.
tasks.prompt: Write four lines spelling RAIN downward by first letters, using only capital letters throughout (punctuation allowed, no lowercase anywhere). Output only the four lines.
tasks.output:
RUSHING WATER FALLS FROM THE SKY.
ALWAYS DRENCHING THE DRY EARTH.
INTO THE SOIL IT SOAKS.
NATURE DRINKS THE FRESH DROPLETS.

tasks.summary: Exactly 3 lines, each starting with Because, each ending with a question mark.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly three lines, each beginning with the word "Because" followed by a space and each ending with a question mark. No blank lines. Output only the three lines.
tasks.output:
Because the stars refuse to shine tonight?
Because we lost the map we drew in youth?
Because the silence speaks the loudest truth?

tasks.summary: Exactly 10 words: an invented proverb.
tasks.prompt: Invent a proverb in exactly 10 words. Words are whitespace-separated tokens. Output only the proverb.
tasks.output: A quiet river carries no warning of the waterfall ahead.

tasks.summary: Exactly 60 words: a eulogy for a houseplant.
tasks.prompt: Write a eulogy for a beloved houseplant in exactly 60 words. Every whitespace-separated token counts as one word. Output only the eulogy.
tasks.output: Dear green companion, you graced my windowsill with quiet, vibrant life. Through dry spells and overwatering, you persevered, unfurling tender leaves toward the morning sun. Your sudden, tragic wilt leaves a dusty, empty pot and a heavy heart. I will deeply miss your silent, soothing presence. Rest in peace, leafy friend. May your compost nourish future blooms.

tasks.summary: A travel ad without the letter t.
tasks.prompt: Write a two-sentence travel advertisement for an island without using the letter "t" anywhere, uppercase or lowercase. Output only the two sentences.
tasks.output: Come explore our calm golden beaches and swim in a warm blue lagoon. Relax under swaying palms as mild ocean breezes soothe your weary mind.

tasks.summary: Describe the night sky without the letter o.
tasks.prompt: Describe the night sky in one to three sentences without using the letter "o" anywhere, uppercase or lowercase. Output only the description.
tasks.output: Darkness blankets the silent earth as tiny silver pinpricks gleam in the deep sky. A pale crescent shines, casting its dim light upon the quiet land. Brilliant stars glitter like dust scattered in the air.

tasks.summary: Eight lines whose first letters spell CRUCIBLE.
tasks.prompt: Write an eight-line poem in which the first letters of the lines spell CRUCIBLE, in order. Output only the eight lines.
tasks.output:
Cold winds whisper through the darkened trees,
Raging storms begin to shake the ground,
Underneath the weight of ancient seas,
Crashing waves produce a solemn sound.
In the fire, the metal starts to glow,
Boundless heat will shape the iron band,
Life is forged within the depths below,
Entering a new and altered land.

tasks.summary: Six lines whose first letters spell WINTER.
tasks.prompt: Write a six-line poem in which the first letters of the lines spell WINTER, in order. Output only the six lines.
tasks.output:
Wind howls through the barren trees,
Ice begins to freeze the lake,
Night descends with freezing breeze,
Trees are shivering in the cold,
Each soft snowflake starts to fall,
Resting on the silent ground.

tasks.summary: Exactly ten lines: a wizard-shop inventory.
tasks.prompt: Write a wizard shop's inventory as exactly ten lines, one item per line. No blank lines, no numbering required. Output only the ten lines.
tasks.output:
Bottled starlight harvested during the winter solstice
A silver cauldron that stirs itself clockwise
Dried phoenix feathers preserved in amber jars
A leather-bound grimoire whispering forgotten languages
Polished obsidian mirrors for scrying distant realms
Essence of thunderstorm captured in crystal vials
A brass astrolabe that maps shifting constellations
Velvet pouches containing powdered unicorn horn
Self-lighting candles that burn with blue flames
A wooden staff carved from a lightning-struck oak

tasks.summary: Seven lines of folk wisdom, each beginning with If.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly seven lines of folk wisdom, each beginning with the word "If" followed by a space. No blank lines. Output only the seven lines.
tasks.output:
If the river runs silent, tread with caution on the bank.
If the hearth fire crackles blue, expect a visitor by night.
If the swallows fly low, prepare your roof for heavy rain.
If you plant in the dark moon, your harvest will be thin.
If a dog barks at nothing, let him guide you past the gate.
If you speak of your sorrow to the wind, it will carry it away.
If the oak sheds its leaves early, the winter will be long.

tasks.summary: A sentence of 10+ words, every word starting with m.
tasks.prompt: Write one sentence of at least ten words in which every word begins with the letter "m". Use no punctuation except commas and one final period. Output only the sentence.
tasks.output: Many mighty, majestic monarchs make massive mountains meet modern, magnificent milestones.

tasks.summary: Four lines, each exactly five words.
tasks.prompt: Write four lines about the tide, each line containing exactly five whitespace-separated words. No blank lines. Output only the four lines.
tasks.output:
The ocean pulls the sand
Away into the deep blue
Then returns with gentle waves
Washing all the shore clean

tasks.summary: Exactly three sentences, each ending with a period.
tasks.prompt: Write about an old key in exactly three sentences. Each sentence ends with a period; use no question marks or exclamation points and no other periods. Output only the three sentences.
tasks.output: Coated in a thick layer of rust, the heavy iron key lay forgotten at the bottom of the dusty drawer. It once unlocked the grand oak doors of a manor that had long since crumbled into ruin. Now, it remained only as a silent keeper of secrets that no living soul would ever discover.

tasks.summary: Three lines spelling FOG, containing no letter e.
tasks.prompt: Write three lines in which the first letters spell FOG downward, and the letter "e" never appears anywhere, uppercase or lowercase. Output only the three lines.
tasks.output:
From a cold harbor,
Onward rolls a gray shroud,
Gloom masks our world.

tasks.summary: Five lines starting with We, each ending in a period.
tasks.prompt: Write exactly five lines, each beginning with the word "We" followed by a space and each ending with a period. No blank lines. Output only the five lines.
tasks.output:
We walk along the quiet shore.
We watch the waves crash on the sand.
We listen to the seagulls cry.
We feel the cold wind on our skin.
We find our peace in this place.

tasks.summary: A warning of exactly eight words, capitals only.
tasks.prompt: Write a warning sign of exactly eight whitespace-separated words using only capital letters (punctuation allowed, no lowercase anywhere). Output only the sign.
tasks.output: DANGER! HIGH VOLTAGE! KEEP OUT! AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY!
